scope_note: QA Witt Tower AR(1)-Baseline Reranking Cert [516]. Primary sources: Rayner et al. (2003) 'Global analyses of sea surface temperature' J. Geophys. Res. 108(D14) DOI 10.1029/2002JD002670 (SST persistence baseline cited by cert [493]); Namias (1952) synoptic blocking baseline cited by cert [492]; this cert's own pure-Python AR(1)/rank-bin Monte Carlo simulation (reproduce_ar1_baseline.py, no external dependency). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): the 'discrimination ladder' documented across certs [490]-[495] ranks domains (rivers, precipitation, temperature, ocean SST, EEG interictal, 1-min FX) by raw n_signal_ratio, which conflates (1) how strongly the domain is ALREADY autocorrelated at lag 1 -- a pre-existing, well-documented fact cited in each cert's own primary sources -- with (2) how much the QA rank-bin operator reveals beyond what that known autocorrelation alone predicts. Re-ranking by (2) instead of raw ratio nearly inverts the published ladder: SST and temperature, presented as 'STRONGEST PERSISTENCE', show excess_ratio 1.05 and 1.01 respectively (essentially zero genuine excess -- a plain AR(1) Gaussian process at the SAME reported lag-1 autocorrelation already produces 4.23x and 3.36x, matching the observed 4.432x and 3.400x almost exactly). EEG interictal, precipitation, rivers, and FX -- ranked lower or 'null' in the original framing -- show substantial genuine excess (1.64x, 1.60x, 1.43x, 1.42x respectively). This does not retract [490]-[495]'s underlying data or n_signal_ratio computations, independently re-verified here as correct; it corrects the interpretive framing that implicitly equated 'highest raw ratio' with 'most novel finding'. Rivers' lag-1 autocorrelation (rho=0.310 pooled) was live-fetched from USGS NWIS log-return data (4 gauges: Potomac 0.385, Hudson 0.203, Missouri 0.306, Eel 0.345) since cert [490]'s fallback record does not store an autocorr field. Checks ARB_SIM_NULL/SST_NEAR_NULL/TEMP_NEAR_NULL/EEG_EXCESS/PRECIP_EXCESS/RIVERS_EXCESS/FX_EXCESS/RERANK_INVERTS/WITNESS; 9 gating checks all PASS. Companion: certs [490],[491],[492],[493],[494],[495] (audits their published discrimination-ladder ranking without retracting their underlying data). Derived 2026-07-04.
